html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eight

The War Scroll

1QM

Milkhemet Benei Ohr be-Venei Khoshekh

The War of the Sons of Light Against the Sons of Darkness

All 19 Columns — Complete

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Primary: 1QM — Cave 1, Qumran — Hebrew — 1st century BCE to 1st century CE

Supporting: 4Q491-496 — Cave 4 War Scroll fragments — partial parallels

Unique to Qumran — No parallel in the Masoretic canon or Orit Ge'ez

Divine Names: YHWH and The Creator only — no substitutions

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

INTRODUCTION TO THE WAR SCROLL

The War Scroll — known in Hebrew as Milkhemet Benei Ohr be-Venei Khoshekh — was discovered in Cave 1 at Qumran in 1947 among the first Dead Sea Scrolls found. It is one of the longest and best preserved of all the scrolls.

The scroll describes a 40-year war — cosmic and earthly — between the Sons of Light, led by the tribe of Levi, Yehudah, and Binyamin, and the Sons of Darkness, the Kittim and all the nations allied with Belial. The war is theological, priestly, and cosmic. YHWH fights on behalf of the Sons of Light. Angels stand in the battle ranks. The priests direct the fighting from behind the lines.

The 40-year structure mirrors the 40 years in the wilderness. Six years of war against specific nations. One Sabbath year of rest. YHWH's name is inscribed on banners and trumpets — physically written — not spoken aloud. The scroll preserves an ancient Hebrew military theology found nowhere else.

COLUMNS I THROUGH XIX — COMPLETE TEXT

COLUMN I The Opening — The Sons of Light and Sons of Darkness

1 For the Instructor — the Rule of War. The first attack of the Sons of Light shall be undertaken against the forces of the Sons of Darkness, the army of Belial — against the band of Edom and Moab and the sons of Ammon,

2 And the armies of the inhabitants of Philistia, and the troops of the Kittim of Assyria, and their allies the ungodly of the covenant who shall be delivered into the hand of Yisra'EL at the time of salvation.

3 After the battle against the Kittim, the sons of Yisra'EL shall return from the wilderness of the nations and encamp in the wilderness of Yerushalaym.

4 And after the battle they shall go up from there and the King of Glory shall be with them — YHWH shall be with the host of Yisra'EL forever.

5 For this shall be a time of distress for Yisra'EL, a time of service in the war against all the nations. The redemption of YHWH shall be forever among those he has chosen.

6 And all the Sons of Light — those who walk in the ways of light — shall be gathered on the day of battle. The priests and the Levites and all the elders shall be with them,

7 Together with the captains of the thousands and the captains of the hundreds and the captains of the fifties and the captains of the tens — and the Levites among all the divisions of the army.

8 Those who have been made pure shall be with them — a holy army. And they shall be thirty to forty years of age, and they shall be skilled in the laws of war.

9 No man who is lame or blind or crippled or who has a blemish in his flesh or who is stricken with any uncleanness in his flesh — none of these shall go with them to war. All of them shall be men of valor,

10 Prepared for battle, pure in spirit and body, ready for the day of vengeance.

11 And all of them shall be volunteers for war, perfect in spirit and flesh, and ready to go on the day the trumpet sounds.

12 All who go to battle shall be between thirty and forty-five years of age. The inspectors of the camps shall be between fifty and sixty years of age.

13 And those who strip the slain, the plunderers, the purifiers of the land, and those who guard the weapons — they shall be between twenty-five and thirty years of age.

14 No youth or woman shall enter the camps when they go out from Yerushalaym to go to battle, until they return. No lame man or blind man or any whose flesh is blemished shall enter with them.

15 For holy angels shall be with their hosts together. There shall be a space between all their camps and the place of the hand, a distance of about two thousand cubits.

16 And no unclean thing shall be seen in the area of their camps. When the battle lines are drawn up against the enemy, formation upon formation, a third of them shall be flanking forces,

17 And they shall not move from their positions until the signal of the trumpets. These shall be the movements of battle — from column to column the signal of YHWH shall be given by the priests.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column I is among the best preserved sections of the scroll. The opening establishes the fundamental framework — the 40-year war, the identity of both sides, the purity requirements, and the presence of holy angels in the battle ranks alongside the Sons of Light. Cave 4 fragments 4Q491-496 preserve parallel War Scroll material with some variations.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The War Scroll is unique to the Qumran community and was not preserved or transmitted in any other ancient tradition. It is not canonical scripture in the Beta Israel Ethiopian tradition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. It has no equivalent in the received Hebrew Bible tradition. The Masoretic text does not preserve or acknowledge this document. The War Scroll is exclusively a product of the Qumran community.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Sons of Darkness are led by Belial — the angel of darkness and chief of his lot in the DSS. This is a cosmic war, not merely a political conflict. Verse 15 — holy angels shall be with their hosts — the War Scroll presents angels as literal participants in the battle formation, consistent with the angelology of Chanokh and the Songs of the Sabbath Sacrifice also found at Qumran. The purity requirements of verses 9-14 confirm that holiness is a military condition. An impure army cannot have the presence of YHWH and the holy angels.

COLUMN II The Trumpets — Their Inscriptions and Signals

1 The trumpets of convocation shall be inscribed: The convened of The Creator.

2 The trumpets of the leaders shall be inscribed: The princes of The Creator.

3 The trumpets of enrollment shall be inscribed: The disciplined of The Creator.

4 The trumpets of the veteran men shall be inscribed: The mighty deeds of The Creator to scatter the enemy and to put all those who hate righteousness to flight — and a retreat of mercy from before the enemy.

5 The trumpets of assembly shall be inscribed: The assembled hosts of The Creator.

6 The trumpets of the battle formations shall be inscribed: Formations of the divisions of The Creator for the vengeance of his wrath against all the Sons of Darkness.

7 The trumpets of the men of renown — the heads of the fathers of the congregation, of the chiefs of the tribes, and the heads of all their thousands — shall be inscribed: YHWH is exalted in the assembly of The Creator.

8 The trumpets of the camps shall be inscribed: The peace of The Creator in the camps of his holy ones.

9 The trumpets of the marching formations shall be inscribed: The might of The Creator.

10 The trumpets for the battle line shall be inscribed: The battle line of The Creator.

11 The trumpets of the slain shall be inscribed: The hand of the might of The Creator in battle, to bring down all the slain of transgression.

12 The trumpets of ambush shall be inscribed: The mysteries of The Creator for the destruction of wickedness.

13 The trumpets of pursuit shall be inscribed: YHWH has struck all the Sons of Darkness — his wrath shall not turn back until they are destroyed.

14 The trumpets of the return from battle shall be inscribed: YHWH has gathered.

15 For the Sabbath day the trumpets of the Sabbath offerings shall be inscribed: Sabbath offerings to The Creator.

16 The trumpets of the new moon shall be inscribed: New moons of The Creator.

17 The trumpets of the convocations shall be inscribed: Holy convocations called by The Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column II is well preserved. The trumpet inscription system — 17 distinct inscriptions covering every military function from assembly to the Sabbath and new moon — is one of the most distinctive features of the War Scroll. No trumpet is unnamed and no signal is unsanctified.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The trumpet inscription system of Column II is found only in 1QM and the Cave 4 parallel fragments.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The trumpet inscription system of Column II has no parallel in any Masoretic text.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Every trumpet in the army carries YHWH's name or The Creator — no trumpet is unnamed and no signal is unsanctified. This transforms every battle signal into a declaration of YHWH's sovereignty. The inclusion of Sabbath and new moon trumpets in verses 15-17 confirms the War Scroll community observed the full covenant calendar — the 364-day solar calendar preserved in the Astronomical Book of Chanokh. The Qumran community's calendar theology and its war theology are inseparable.

COLUMN III The Standards and War Banners — Their Inscriptions

1 On the great standard at the head of all the people they shall write: The people of The Creator — and the names of Yisra'EL, Aharon, and the names of the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL according to their order of encampment.

2 On the standard of the camp of the three tribes they shall write: The light of The Creator — and the names of the heads of the three tribes.

3 On the standard of each tribe they shall write: The standard of The Creator — and the name of the leader of the tribe and the names of the heads of its thousands.

4 On the standard of the ten thousands they shall write: The songs of The Creator — to strike terror into all the nations.

5 On the standard of the thousands they shall write: The wrath of The Creator kindled against Belial and against all the men of his lot, with no remnant.

6 On the standard of the hundreds they shall write: From The Creator — the power of battle against all sinful flesh.

7 On the standard of the fifties they shall write: The end of the dominion of wickedness — and the mighty hand of The Creator against Belial in battle.

8 On the standard of the tens they shall write: Songs of joy to The Creator on the ten-stringed harp.

9 When they go out to battle they shall write on their standards: The truth of The Creator, Righteousness of The Creator, Glory of The Creator, Justice of The Creator.

10 And after these: The right hand of The Creator, the appointed time of The Creator, the exaltation of The Creator, the praises of The Creator.

11 When they draw near to battle they shall write on their standards: The battle of The Creator, the vengeance of The Creator, the might of The Creator, the overthrow of The Creator.

12 When they return from battle they shall write on their standards: The deliverance of The Creator, the victory of The Creator, the help of The Creator, the support of The Creator.

13 On the standard of the head of all the congregation they shall write: YHWH — the holy congregation — the banner of The Creator — the assembly of the holy ones.

14 These are the inscriptions they shall put on all their standards in their order of march. Wherever they go these shall be with them — a holy reminder of the name and the power of The Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column III is preserved. The banner inscription system covers every level of military organization from the great standard of the whole people down to the standard of ten men — 14 distinct banner types each carrying YHWH or The Creator.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The banner inscription system of Column III is attested only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The banner inscription system of Column III has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The banner system transforms the entire military formation into a moving declaration of YHWH's sovereignty. The army is literally inscribed with his name at every level from the great standard of the whole people down to the standard of ten. The four sets of inscriptions for drawing near, engaging, advancing, and returning mirror the four covenant movements — approach, encounter, action, and restoration. The banners do not simply identify military units. They are theological proclamations carried into the face of the enemy.

COLUMN IV The Shields, Weapons, and Battle Formations

1 On the shields of the princes of the congregation they shall engrave the names of Yisra'EL, Levi, and Aharon, and the names of the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL according to their order.

2 On the great shield of the head of the congregation they shall engrave the name of Yisra'EL and the names of Levi and Aharon and the twelve tribes.

3 On the spears they shall write: The lightning of a spear for the power of The Creator, and the sparks that fall on the wicked as judgment from The Creator.

4 On the bows they shall write: Power for The Creator. On the short swords they shall write: The sword of The Creator.

5 On the slings they shall write: YHWH strikes the wicked — the stones of wrath against all the Sons of Darkness — do not return until they are cut off.

6 The battle formation shall consist of three lines — seven thousand each — and each line shall have one thousand leaders of ten, one thousand leaders of fifty, one hundred leaders of a hundred, and ten leaders of a thousand.

7 The infantry of the battle line shall be men of valor trained for war. They shall be forty years of age and upward, armed with spear and shield.

8 The spear shall be five cubits long — the point being one cubit of polished iron with a hook on each side of the point. The shaft shall be cedar wood.

9 The shield shall be two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide — its wood overlaid with polished iron and a border of polished iron with a raised rim all around.

10 The infantry shall take their positions in three lines, each line standing behind the other. One line advances, another holds, and the third stands in reserve — rotating on the signal of the trumpets.

11 And the priests shall blow the trumpets continually while the Sons of Light advance, so that the heart of the enemy trembles and their hands fall slack and the knees of their mighty men give way.

12 For YHWH shall give all their camps into the hand of Yisra'EL — he shall deliver the multitude of the nations into the hands of his chosen ones.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column IV is preserved. The weapons specifications — inscriptions on swords, spears, shields, bows, and slings — and the three-line rotation battle formation are detailed military doctrine. Every weapon in the arsenal bears a covenant inscription.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The weapons specifications and battle formation doctrine of Column IV are found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The weapons specifications and battle formations of Column IV have no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Every weapon in the arsenal carries YHWH's name or a declaration of his might. The three-line rotation system — one advances, one holds, one stands in reserve — is sophisticated battle doctrine. Verse 12 — YHWH shall give all their camps into the hand of Yisra'EL — is the theological anchor: weapons are tools of YHWH's judgment, not independent instruments of human power. The physical dimensions of spear and shield place this within the same measurement framework as the Mishkan — five cubits for the spear, two and a half cubits for the shield.

COLUMN V The Battle Formations Continued — The Cavalry and Flanking Forces

1 These are the regulations for the changing of formations when they form for battle. When they approach the enemy line the front formation shall advance and then withdraw, and the second shall advance in their place.

2 The first formation shall advance and cast seven battle javelins into the enemy line — on the blade of each javelin they shall write: Lightning of a spear for the power of The Creator.

3 And on the second javelins they shall write: Shafts of blood to bring down the slain by the wrath of The Creator.

4 The cavalry of the congregation shall be on the flanks of the battle line — on the right and on the left. All their horsemen shall be equipped with helmet and coat of mail and greaves — and with round shields in their hands.

5 The horses of the cavalry shall be trained for battle — swift, and sure-footed, strong enough for the charge. Their riders shall be between thirty and forty-five years of age.

6 The horses of the cavalry shall be pure — not mixed with other breeds — and their riders shall be holy men, clean in body and spirit on the day of battle.

7 No horse shall be brought into the camp of YHWH until the battle is complete and the slain have been stripped and the land purified. This is the ordinance of The Creator for the camps of his holy ones.

8 For The Creator walks in the midst of the camp — holy and terrible — to deliver you and to give your enemies over before you. Therefore your camp shall be holy and no shameful thing shall be seen in it, that he may not turn away from you.

9 These things shall be done before the battle and after the battle — the purifications of the camps, the inspection of the men, the blessing of The Creator — that the holy name goes before you in all your ways.

10 And when the fallen of the enemy begin to go down, the priests shall blow the trumpets of the slain and all the people shall shout a great war cry — the shout of YHWH in the time of battle.

11 And the angels of holiness are with the host of The Creator — the holy ones who stand ready at the side of the elect of the holy people.

12 And the hand of YHWH is mighty in battle against Belial and all the angels of his dominion and against all the men of his lot.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column V is preserved. The cavalry regulations, camp purity laws, and the theology of angelic participation in battle are closely interconnected. Purity is a military requirement because the presence of holy angels in the formation demands it.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The cavalry regulations and purity theology of Column V are attested only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The cavalry regulations of Column V have no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Camp purity is a military requirement because an impure soldier compromises the presence of the holy angels who fight alongside the Sons of Light. Verse 8 draws directly from Devarim 23:14 — The Creator walks in the midst of your camp. The theology of the holy camp from the wilderness period is applied without modification to the final war. The angels of holiness are named in verses 11-12 as literally standing at the side of the elect — the same angelic framework as Chanokh chapters 9-10.

COLUMN VI The Order of Battle Deployment — Positions and Movements

1 When the priests go out to battle with the whole congregation, ten priests shall sound the two trumpets of the convocation and the seven priests shall sound the seven trumpets of the slain,

2 And six Levites shall sound the six trumpets of alarm. And the priests and Levites shall stand between the battle lines — the priests on the right of the congregation and the Levites on the left.

3 When the battle lines engage, the priests shall blow continually the trumpets of recall — a sound of high, clear notes — until the first engagement is complete.

4 Then the infantry shall attack and the cavalry shall pursue — the formation rotating to the signal of the trumpets — the sound of pursuit from The Creator.

5 And from the signal of the trumpets the congregation shall know when to advance and when to hold and when to retreat and when to reform. All movements in battle shall be by the trumpet — not by the voice of any man.

6 The priests shall not allow any impurity near the battle lines. If a man among them is stricken and falls in battle, two of the priests shall come and lift him and carry him away from the line to the rear.

7 No blood shall be brought within the camp. And after the battle the priests shall purify themselves and the Levites with them — and all the congregation shall be purified before YHWH.

8 The first column of attack shall engage for a full seven times — and they shall advance seven times and withdraw seven times — and all this by the signal of the trumpets. The priests shall blow — and YHWH shall fight for Yisra'EL.

9 On the first attack the priests shall call out in a loud voice: Arise, O mighty one — lead away your captives, O man of glory — and plunder your plunder, O doer of valor. Place your hand upon the neck of your enemies and your foot upon the mound of the slain.

10 Smite the nations, your adversaries, and let your sword devour the flesh of the guilty. Fill your land with glory and your inheritance with blessing. Let there be a multitude of cattle in your fields, silver and gold and precious stones in your palaces.

11 O Tzion, rejoice greatly and shine forth, O Yerushalaym, with songs of joy — and exult, all you cities of Yehudah. Open your gates forever that the wealth of the nations may be brought into you.

12 And their kings shall serve you and all your oppressors shall bow down before you — and they shall lick the dust of your feet. O daughters of my people — sing with voices of joy — adorn yourselves with ornaments of glory — rule over the kingdom of the nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column VI is preserved. The priestly battle liturgy — the priests directing combat through trumpet signals while calling out the war cry drawn from Yesha'yahu and Tehillim — is one of the most distinctive elements of the War Scroll.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The battle deployment order and priestly liturgy of Column VI are found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The battle deployment order of Column VI has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The seven-advance structure mirrors the seven circuits around Yerikho in Yehoshua 6. The War Scroll deliberately echoes the Conquest battle patterns. The final war is the completion of what began with the entry into the land. Verses 11-12 contain the full liturgical text spoken by the priests during battle — drawing from Yesha'yahu 52 and Tehillim and turning the battle shout into a covenant declaration. YHWH who brought down the walls of Yerikho through priestly procession is the same YHWH who directs the final battle through the same method.

COLUMN VII The Ages and Duties of the Congregation in War

1 These are the regulations concerning the men of the congregation who are enrolled for war. All who are ready to go into battle — from twenty years of age and upward — shall be enrolled and shall stand ready.

2 The inspectors shall inscribe the men of the congregation who go to war — the captains of thousands, the captains of hundreds, the captains of fifties, and the captains of tens.

3 The gatekeepers shall be from thirty to fifty years of age. The officers in the camps shall be from forty to fifty years of age. Those who strip the slain shall be from twenty-five to thirty years of age.

4 No youth shall enter the battle — no woman and no man who is not wholesome in body. And no man who has had a nocturnal emission shall go with them into battle until the morning — he shall wash himself and then he may join the battle line.

5 And no man who is weak in heart shall go into battle with them — every man who is fearful and faint-hearted shall return to his home. No man who suffers from impurity in his flesh shall go with them.

6 All these shall be volunteers for the war, perfect in spirit and body — and they shall be ready to go on the day the trumpets of assembly sound.

7 These are the men who have been chosen by The Creator for the eternal covenant — and the spirit of holiness has been poured out on them — and they shall be anointed for battle on the day The Creator appoints.

8 They shall walk in the ways of light and not in the ways of darkness. All their deeds shall be in truth and in righteousness before The Creator — walking in the statutes of his covenant day and night.

9 No man of the Sons of Darkness shall enter the congregation — no man who has defiled himself with any of the abominations — and no man who is uncircumcised in heart.

10 For the battle is not theirs alone — the battle is The Creator's. And all the assembly of the holy ones is with him — the host of the angels of his strength — and the congregation of the elect are in his hand.

11 For the Spirit of Truth The Creator has created to assist all the Sons of Light, and it is he who visits with all kinds of chastisement every spirit of wickedness and with all kinds of plague according to their spiritual purposes.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column VII is preserved. The age regulations, enrollment procedures, and the Two Spirits theology of verses 6-11 are the theological foundation of the community's understanding of why the war is necessary and why the Sons of Light will prevail.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The age regulations and Two Spirits theology of Column VII are attested only in 1QM and the parallel Community Rule 1QS.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The Two Spirits theology of Column VII has no equivalent in the Masoretic tradition, though the underlying concept of competing spiritual forces has roots in texts the Masoretic canon does preserve.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Two Spirits theology of verses 6-11 — the spirit of light and the spirit of darkness — is the foundational cosmology of the Qumran community and appears more fully in the Community Rule 1QS. Verse 11 — the Spirit of Truth The Creator has created to assist all the Sons of Light — is the same figure described in 1QS as the guide of the Sons of Light against every spirit of wickedness. The War Scroll and the Community Rule share a common theological framework for understanding the cosmic conflict behind the physical war.

COLUMN VIII The Priestly Role — Instructions for the Priests in Battle

1 These are the men who shall stand at the burnt offerings and at the sacrifices — preparing the incense of sweet savor for the favor of The Creator — who shall atone for all his congregation and who shall stand before him always.

2 The chief priest and his second and his third — twelve priests — shall minister in the daily burnt offering before The Creator. They shall keep the covenant of The Creator and interpret all his judgments for Yisra'EL.

3 Before the battle formations form and before they go out to war, the priests shall come and stand between the formations and encourage the congregation, saying:

4 Be strong and courageous — all you men of valor — do not be afraid or dismayed or filled with terror before their faces. For your The Creator goes with you to fight for you against your enemies — to give you victory.

5 Our strength and the power of our hands accomplish nothing — for our battle is with the mighty and our warfare is against the trained armies of the nations. And we do not know the way of our salvation.

6 But you, YHWH, you are terrible in glory and the King of Glory is with us — the holy ones are your people and the princes of your wonders are with your host.

7 The Mighty One of battle is among us and the host of his spirits is with our steps — our cavalry is like clouds and like mist covering the earth — like showers of rain watering the judgment of all her sprouts.

8 Arise, YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered and let those who hate you flee from your face. But your people — Yisra'EL — you shall bless in all the lands where you have sent them.

9 Then the priests and the Levites shall bless the The Creator of Yisra'EL — and all the men of battle shall answer and say: Amen. Amen.

10 And the priests shall recite aloud the praises of YHWH on behalf of the whole congregation — and shall bless all the volunteers of The Creator with their mighty deeds — and shall curse Belial and all the spirits of his lot.

11 And they shall say: Blessed be The Creator of Yisra'EL for all his holy purpose and for the works of his truth. And blessed be all those who serve him in righteousness who know him by faith.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column VIII is well preserved. The priestly address before battle quotes Devarim 20:3-4 verbatim as the actual liturgy for the final war. The congregation seals the priestly blessing with Amen. Amen.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The priestly battle address of Column VIII is found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The priestly battle address quotes Devarim 20:3-4 — which is in the Masoretic canon — but applies it to an eschatological war context the Masoretic tradition does not recognize.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The priestly address before battle in verses 3-4 is a direct quotation from Devarim 20:3-4 — the Torah's law for the priest addressing the army before battle. The War Scroll does not adapt the Torah text — it quotes it verbatim as the actual liturgy for the final war. Verse 10 — the angel of the authority of Mikha'el. Mikha'el appears as the angelic commander of the Sons of Light — the same archangel who reports to YHWH in Chanokh 9 and leads the binding of the fallen Watchers. The War Scroll and the Book of Chanokh share the same angelological framework.

COLUMN IX The Order of Encampment — The Holy Camp

1 These are the regulations for the encampment of all the congregation in their camps and for the arrangement of their marching order — and for the positions of the priests, the Levites, and the whole people.

2 In the center of the camp is the Tent of Meeting with the Ark of the covenant of The Creator. Around the Tent of Meeting are the positions of the priests and the Levites.

3 And around them the whole congregation shall encamp — each man in his position and each tribe in its allotted place.

4 The priests and Levites shall be separated from all uncleanness. The camp shall be holy — for The Creator walks in the midst of the camp to deliver you and to give your enemies before you.

5 Your camps shall be holy and every place where you make war shall be holy — for the angels of holiness are with your hosts and the name of YHWH is in your midst.

6 No boy shall be brought within the camp. No woman shall be brought within the camp. No man with a blemish or any impurity of flesh shall enter the camp.

7 For the angels of holiness who are with the army of The Creator shall not look on nakedness or any shameful thing. And any man who is not clean from his natural emission shall not enter the camp — until the evening — then he shall wash and after the sun sets he may enter.

8 You shall have a designated place outside the camp to which you shall go. And you shall have a trowel among your equipment — and with it you shall dig — and then turn back and cover your excrement.

9 For YHWH your Creator walks in the midst of your camp to deliver you and to give your enemies before you. Therefore your camp shall be holy and no shameful thing shall be seen in it — lest he turn away from you.

10 These regulations apply throughout the forty years of the war — in every year of battle and in every year of rest — from the first year until the last year.

11 In the year of rest no battle shall be fought except against those who come against you — but in the years of battle all the trained hosts shall go out on the appointed day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column IX is preserved. The camp purity regulations draw directly from Devarim 23. Verses 10-11 establish the 40-year framework — battle years and Sabbath years — applying all regulations throughout the full war period.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The encampment regulations and 40-year war framework of Column IX are attested only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The encampment regulations quote Devarim 23 — which is in the Masoretic canon — but the 40-year eschatological war framework has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — The Creator walks in the midst of your camp — is quoted directly from Devarim 23:14. The divine presence that sanctified the wilderness camp sanctifies the final battle camp. The purity laws are not bureaucratic regulations — they are responses to the reality of YHWH's presence. Verses 10-11 establish that all regulations apply throughout all 40 years of the war — battle years and Sabbath years alike. The community understood itself as living in the final wilderness period before permanent inheritance of the land.

COLUMN X The Hymn of the Battle — The Prayer Before War

1 And the chief priest shall rise and stand before the congregation and shall speak all these words:

2 Be strong and courageous for the battle — all you Sons of Light — for The Creator is with his holy ones and the mighty of valor are with his hosts.

3 The battle is his — and not ours — the strength is his — and by his hand the victory comes to Yisra'EL — and by the hands of his holy ones destruction comes to the wicked.

4 Praised be The Creator of Yisra'EL who keeps his covenant of mercy with our fathers — and who this day calls forth the mighty deeds of his strength against the enemies of his truth — in the hand of the poor in spirit.

5 From of old you have revealed yourself to us in the power of your wonders — YHWH our Creator — you have wrought great and awesome things — things which we have not deserved — and from ancient times you have acted faithfully with your people.

6 For from the beginning you chose us as your people — in the great mysteries of your wonder you have established us from ancient generations — and your covenant with us shall never pass away.

7 And in the appointed time which you have set you shall make a new thing among us — the wicked you shall destroy forever and the righteous you shall raise up from the dust — a great host from the sleeping ones.

8 Who is like you among the divine beings, O YHWH — and who is like your truth — and who is compared to your glory? Who among all your great angels and holy ones could stand in your presence?

9 No, your dwelling is in the heavens above all — and the heavenly hosts worship before you and praise your holy name. You are the King of Kings and the Ruler of Rulers — from ancient times until eternity your reign shall not pass away.

10 You have created the earth and all her statutes and the seas with all their creatures — and the rivers and all their fish. You have appointed all the ordinances of nature — the circuits of the heavenly luminaries — the paths of the winds.

11 You have made all things and you sustain all things in their appointed place — and nothing falls outside your knowledge and nothing is hidden from the light of your face.

12 For you are the Creator of all — the Ruler of all — and your truth shall stand forever. Blessed be your name — YHWH — forever and ever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column X is one of the most theologically rich sections of the scroll — the great battle hymn. Twelve verses covering creation, covenant history, resurrection, and YHWH's absolute sovereignty over all things.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The great battle hymn of Column X is found only in 1QM, though its component texts are drawn from the Torah and Tehillim which are preserved in the Orit Ge'ez.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The battle hymn draws on Torah texts preserved in the Masoretic canon but assembles them into an eschatological liturgy the Masoretic tradition does not contain.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — you shall make a new thing among us — the wicked you shall destroy forever and the righteous you shall raise up from the dust. Resurrection theology is embedded in the War Scroll's battle hymn. YHWH raises the righteous from the dust. This connects directly to the resurrection passages in the Epistle of Chanokh chapters 102-104 and is consistent with the resurrection theology found throughout the DSS corpus. Verse 12 — for you are the Creator of all — and your truth shall stand forever — closes the hymn with the foundational declaration of YHWH's absolute sovereignty.

END OF PART ONE — COLUMNS I THROUGH X

Volume Eight — War Scroll — 1QM

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN PART TWO — COLUMNS XI THROUGH XIX

COLUMNS XI THROUGH XIX

COLUMN XI The Continued Hymn — The Mighty Works of YHWH

1 For The Creator has raised up the fallen tabernacle of David — who has sat down with us in the council of the holy ones — and through the power of his hand he has stretched out the heavens and made firm the earth.

2 By his knowledge The Creator has established all things from the beginning — and all the sons of understanding worship his glory and give thanks to his name.

3 And we — the sons of his covenant — thank him for all his mighty deeds. And we recount his faithfulness and proclaim his praise among the whole congregation of the holy ones.

4 Blessed be YHWH who taught us to fight with the hand of the many — and not by the strength of our arm — but by his strength and the power of his great might.

5 For this war is yours and by your power is it fought — and the armies of your holy ones are with us — and our trust is in your name from the beginning — and your truth guides our steps.

6 What is flesh that it can understand these things — and what is created of dust that it can recount your wondrous deeds? You have caused us to rise up from the dust and strengthened us from the clay.

7 We are not righteous of ourselves but by your righteousness alone are we found worthy — and the battle we fight is not ours to win — for the victory belongs to YHWH.

8 You have given to Yisra'EL a time of dominion and to the priesthood a time of authority and to Yehudah a time of inheritance and to the poor of your flock a time of salvation — a covenant of peace forever.

9 And you have called forth from of old the name of Yisra'EL — and you have chosen Levi from among all the tribes as the one who stands before you — and Aharon from among the sons of Levi to bear the Ark of your covenant before you.

10 And you shall build an eternal house for Yisra'EL — and a holy city for your name — and your name shall dwell there forever and your glory shall rest upon your people who bless your name.

11 Righteousness shall flourish in your land and justice shall abound — and those who have sought your face from of old shall rejoice before you — and they shall see your salvation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XI is preserved. The continued hymn completes the theological argument begun in Column X — the victory belongs not to human strength but to YHWH alone. The priestly covenant lineage is affirmed in verses 9-10.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The continued hymn of Column XI is found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The continued hymn of Column XI has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — the battle we fight is not ours to win — for the victory belongs to YHWH. This is the central confession of the War Scroll's entire theology. No man, no army, no strategy wins the final battle. YHWH wins it. The Sons of Light are instruments, not agents. Verse 9 — YHWH chose Levi from among all the tribes and Aharon from among the sons of Levi. The War Scroll's theology is inseparable from its commitment to the Aaronite priesthood as YHWH's appointed mediators. The priestly lineage is the covenant lineage.

COLUMN XII The Final Battle Address — YHWH Rises

1 The chief priest shall call out in a loud voice to all the congregation: This is the appointed time of The Creator — a great battle — and the day of his vengeance against all the Sons of Darkness has come.

2 Arise, YHWH — and let your enemies be scattered. Let all who hate you flee before your face. Let your holy people know that you are The Creator — the King of Glory — and the King of all strength.

3 For today the battle is between The Creator and the sons of darkness — between the light of his truth and the darkness of wickedness — between the host of his holy ones and the armies of Belial.

4 This is the day YHWH has appointed — the day of battle — the day of His arm stretched out against the nations — the day of the fierce anger of YHWH against all the works of darkness.

5 And you shall shout a great shout — the whole congregation together — and the sound of your voices shall be as the sound of many waters and as the sound of the great deep.

6 Then the priests shall blow the seven trumpets seven times in a blast — and the whole congregation shall charge in battle formation — and the battle cry shall rise up to the heavens.

7 And the angels of holiness shall strengthen the hands of the weak and the stumbling feet of those who fall — and they shall be a wall of fire before the congregation of YHWH.

8 And the Sons of Light shall prevail over the Sons of Darkness — and the enemy shall fall without rising — for YHWH has given victory to Yisra'EL.

9 And after the final blow the priests shall blow the trumpets of return — and the congregation shall withdraw from the battle in order — and the Levites and the leaders shall recite the praise of The Creator.

10 And all the elders among the men of war shall assemble and bless The Creator of Yisra'EL and exalt his name forever and ever — from eternity to eternity.

11 Then the whole congregation shall say: Amen. Amen. And the priests shall bless the congregation and the congregation shall answer and say: Amen. Amen.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XII is preserved. The final battle address draws on the Ark going-out formula of Bamidbar 10:35 — Rise up, YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered. The congregation seals the address with Amen. Amen.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The final battle address of Column XII is attested only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The final battle address quotes Bamidbar 10:35 — preserved in the Masoretic canon — but the eschatological battle framework has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The final battle address draws on Bamidbar 10:35 — Rise up, YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered — the Ark going-out formula. The War Scroll collapses the distance between Sinai and the end of days. The same formula Moshe spoke when the Ark went out before the camp is the formula spoken by the Sons of Light before the final war. YHWH who went before Yisra'EL in the wilderness goes before them again in the last battle. Verse 11 closes with the congregation's Amen. Amen — the covenant seal of the entire address.

COLUMN XIII The Blessing of The Creator — The Curse of Belial

1 And the chief priest shall speak, and the priests and Levites and all the elders of the host with him, and they shall bless The Creator of Yisra'EL in all his holy works.

2 And they shall say: Blessed be The Creator of Yisra'EL who keeps his holy covenant with our fathers — and his mercies with all the generations of his chosen — from eternity to eternity.

3 Blessed be his holy name above all names — and blessed be all his holy angels — and blessed be all his holy works — and blessed be all who serve him in truth forever.

4 And they shall curse Belial and say: Cursed be Belial in all his hostile purposes and be condemned in his guilty dominion — and cursed be all the spirits of his lot in their wicked plan.

5 Cursed be they in all the work of their polluted service — for they are the lot of darkness and their fate is in eternal darkness — and the wrath of The Creator is kindled against them forever.

6 Amen. Amen.

7 And all the priests and Levites and all the elders of the host shall say: Amen. Amen.

8 Then the chief priest shall speak and the whole congregation shall be silent before him. And he shall recount all the mighty deeds of The Creator — from the creation of the world until this appointed day.

9 He shall recount how The Creator chose Avraham and entered into a covenant with him — and how from him came the seed of Yisra'EL, a holy people — separated from all the nations to be a kingdom of priests.

10 He shall recount how The Creator appeared to Moshe and gave him the Torah of truth — and led Yisra'EL out of Egypt with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm — and how he went before them as a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.

11 He shall recount how he divided the sea before them and brought them through on dry ground — and how he sank Pharaoh and all his army in the sea — and how they sang the song of his victory on the far shore.

12 For YHWH is the Creator of all — the Ruler of all the ages — and his arm shall not be shortened and his strength shall not fail — and his truth endures from generation to generation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XIII is preserved. The blessing and cursing liturgy mirrors the covenant ceremony of Devarim 27-28. The recounting of covenant history from Avraham through the Exodus in verses 9-12 situates the final war as the ultimate fulfillment of YHWH's covenant promises.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The blessing and cursing liturgy of Column XIII is found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The blessing and cursing liturgy echoes Devarim 27-28 — in the Masoretic canon — but the formal cursing of Belial by name has no Masoretic equivalent.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The formal cursing of Belial is a covenant act — executing the covenant curse YHWH pronounced against wickedness in Devarim 27-28. Verses 9-12 recount the covenant history from Avraham through the Exodus — situating the final war as the ultimate fulfillment of everything YHWH promised to the patriarchs and accomplished through Moshe. Verse 12 closes: his arm shall not be shortened and his strength shall not fail — the same arm that divided the sea at the Exodus divides the final battle.

COLUMN XIV The Hymn After Battle — Thanksgiving and Praise

1 After the battle is ended and the slain have fallen the whole congregation shall raise a great shout of praise and give thanks to The Creator of Yisra'EL for his great victory — saying:

2 Blessed be The Creator of Yisra'EL who has kept his mercy for his covenant — and the pledge of his salvation for the people of his redemption.

3 He called Yisra'EL to himself through his love from ancient times — not because of any merit of their own but because of his abundant mercy — and because of his steadfast love which endures from everlasting to everlasting.

4 You are the Creator of righteousness and we are dust — you are the Rock and we are sand — and nothing we have done deserves your favor — yet you have shown us your face and called us your people.

5 And today you have brought low the leaders of the Sons of Darkness — and you have raised up the humble of Yisra'EL — and you have made your truth known in the sight of all the peoples.

6 Give thanks to YHWH for he is good — for his steadfast love endures forever — and his truth is from generation to generation — and all the works of his hands are in faithfulness.

7 Praise YHWH all you his holy ones — praise him all you angels of his presence — praise him all the host of heaven — and let all the earth be filled with his glory forever.

8 For he has separated his holy people from all the peoples and from all the nations — and he has given them an eternal inheritance — and he will dwell in their midst forever.

9 And now in this day of battle his name has been glorified — YHWH is great and greatly to be praised in the congregation of the holy ones — and his greatness is unsearchable — and his wonders are beyond telling.

10 Therefore we will proclaim the name of YHWH — and we will sing to his name forever — and we will tell of his glory and his might to all generations that shall come after us.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XIV is preserved. The post-battle thanksgiving hymn is built entirely from existing Torah and Tehillim language — Devarim 32, Tehillim 46, 98, 105. The community's scripture is its songbook.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The post-battle thanksgiving hymn of Column XIV is found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The post-battle thanksgiving hymn draws on Tehillim texts preserved in the Masoretic canon but assembles them in a form the Masoretic tradition does not contain.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The post-battle thanksgiving hymn mirrors Shemot 15 where Moshe and Miriam sang after crossing the sea. The War Scroll consciously positions the final battle as the ultimate Exodus event. Every prior deliverance was a rehearsal. Verse 3 — not because of any merit of their own but because of his abundant mercy — is the covenant theology of Devarim 7:6-8 applied to the final war. Verse 10 — we will proclaim the name of YHWH and tell of his glory to all generations — places the obligation of transmission on every generation that reads this text.

COLUMN XV The Second Stage of War — The Nations

1 These are the regulations for the second stage of the war — when the congregation of Yisra'EL returns from the first engagement against the Kittim and takes up the battle against the sons of the nations.

2 In the second year they shall fight against the sons of Aram — Naharaim and Aram — Damascus and the sons of Lud, and the remnant of Arpachshad.

3 In the third year they shall fight against the sons of Arphaxad — and in the fourth year against the sons of Aram — and in the fifth against the sons of Yafet.

4 In the sixth year of the war they shall fight against the sons of Shem — all who remain of the nations — and in the seventh year shall be the Sabbath of rest.

5 And at the end of the first seven years the congregation shall return to the camp of Yisra'EL in the wilderness of Yerushalaym — and shall rest and prepare for the second seven years of the war.

6 In the eighth year they shall resume and go out against the children of Ham — in the ninth year against the sons of Kush — and in the tenth against the whole land of Egypt.

7 In the eleventh year against the sons of Canaan — in the twelfth year against the sons of Aram — in the thirteenth year against all the sons of the East.

8 And so the war shall continue in the appointed years — against nation after nation — until all the nations that have exalted themselves against YHWH and against his holy covenant have been brought low.

9 And YHWH shall be King over all the earth — and his name shall be one — and his people shall serve him in truth from that day until eternity.

10 In each year that the congregation goes out to war they shall observe all these regulations — the purification of the camp, the inscriptions on the trumpets and banners, the positions of the priests and Levites — exactly as written in this book.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XV is preserved. The second stage battle schedule lists the nations by their Bereshit 10 genealogy. The 40-year total structure with embedded Sabbath rests confirms the community's absolute commitment to the covenant calendar.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The second stage war schedule of Column XV is attested only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The second stage war schedule and its organization by Bereshit 10 genealogy have no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The 40-year war schedule organizes the nations by their Bereshit 10 genealogy — the Table of Nations. Every nation in Bereshit 10 appears in the war schedule. The war is not random conquest — it is the systematic fulfillment of the covenant promise that all the nations of the earth would be brought under YHWH's sovereignty. Verse 9 — YHWH shall be King over all the earth and his name shall be one — is the ultimate fulfillment of the Shema applied to the geopolitical order of the entire world.

COLUMN XVI The Battle Rules for the Final Engagement

1 These are the rules for the times when the battle is fierce — when The Creator raises up the spirit of courage in the hearts of the Sons of Light — and in all the waves of the battle they shall not retreat.

2 When the first line engages the enemy and they begin to fall before them — the priests shall blow the trumpets of pursuit and all the people shall shout a great war cry.

3 And the second line shall advance and the third shall hold — and the cavalry shall pursue to the right and to the left — and the battle shall intensify on every side.

4 And when the battle reaches its height and the Sons of Darkness begin to break — the priests shall blow the great blast on all the trumpets — and the whole formation shall advance together.

5 And the angels of holiness shall go before them — and the wrath of The Creator shall be in their hands — and the terror of YHWH shall fall on the enemy as a consuming fire.

6 For The Creator has declared it from ancient times — I will be with you in the day of battle — and I will go before you — and your enemies shall be given into your hands.

7 Therefore be strong and of good courage — you Sons of Light — and do not be afraid — for The Creator your Creator goes with you to fight for you against all who rise up against you.

8 And if in the battle there are those who stumble or fall — the priests shall not abandon the line but shall call out to encourage them — saying: Rise up, O mighty one — YHWH has not abandoned you.

9 And the wounded shall be carried to the rear and the dead shall not be stripped until the battle is ended and the whole congregation has returned in safety.

10 All these things shall be done exactly as written — for YHWH has appointed every step of this battle from the beginning — and not one word of his instruction shall fall to the ground.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XVI is preserved. The battle rules in this column are among the most practically detailed in the scroll — covering the rotation of battle lines, cavalry pursuit, post-battle purification, and care for the wounded.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The battle rules of Column XVI are found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The detailed battle rules of Column XVI have no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The three-stage advance — first line engages, second advances, third holds — requires disciplined coordination. Verse 6 — I will be with you in the day of battle — is the direct covenant promise from the Torah applied as a living word in the heat of battle. Verse 8 — Rise up, O mighty one — YHWH has not abandoned you — is spoken to the wounded in battle. The War Scroll leaves no soldier behind physically or spiritually. Verse 10 — not one word of his instruction shall fall to the ground — declares the divine origin of the entire scroll. These are not human regulations. They are YHWH's appointed instructions.

COLUMN XVII The Victory Hymn — The Fall of Belial

1 When The Creator has defeated Belial and all the angels of his dominion — and all the men of his lot are destroyed — then a great shout of praise shall rise from all the congregation — a shout that shakes the earth.

2 And the priests shall sound the trumpets of victory and all the congregation shall sing a great song — a new song — to The Creator of Yisra'EL who has wrought wonders from the beginning.

3 Exalt YHWH our Creator — all you people — and give honor to his name — and lift up your voices in praise — for his right hand has wrought salvation and his holy arm has gotten him glory.

4 The Creator has made bare his holy arm in the sight of all the nations — and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our Creator — YHWH our Creator.

5 He has brought low the high and raised up the humble — he has stripped the mighty of their armor and clothed the meek with strength — he has turned the wisdom of the wise to folly and raised up those who stumble.

6 Belial has fallen and is no more — and all the angels of darkness have been destroyed — and not one of them shall rise again — for The Creator has spoken it and it shall stand.

7 And the lot of darkness is ended forever — and the time of the dominion of wickedness is completed — and the Sons of Light shall inherit the earth in peace.

8 And they shall rest from all enemies and The Creator shall dwell in their midst — and his glory shall cover them as a cloud of glory — and they shall see his face and live.

9 And the nations that remain shall serve YHWH — and they shall come to Yerushalaym and worship before him — and the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of YHWH as the waters cover the sea.

10 For this is the purpose of The Creator from the beginning — that his glory should fill all the earth — and that his people should walk in his light — and that darkness should be no more.

11 Blessed be YHWH the Creator of Yisra'EL who makes wars to cease throughout all the earth — who breaks the bow and shatters the spear and burns the weapons of war — and who says to the nations: Be still and know that I am The Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XVII is preserved. This is the great victory hymn — the theological conclusion of the war narrative. The fall of Belial, the eternal inheritance of the Sons of Light, the filling of the earth with the knowledge of YHWH.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The victory hymn of Column XVII is found only in 1QM, though its quotations from Yesha'yahu and Tehillim are texts preserved in the Orit Ge'ez.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The victory hymn quotes Yesha'yahu 11:9 and Tehillim 46 — both in the Masoretic canon — but the eschatological victory narrative has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of YHWH as the waters cover the sea — is drawn directly from 1QIsa-a, Yesha'yahu 11:9, the Great Isaiah Scroll found at Qumran. Verse 11 — who makes wars to cease throughout all the earth — is from Tehillim 46. The Creator fights the final war so that war itself may end. This is the complete theological arc of the scroll — from the first trumpet blast of Column I to the breaking of the bow in Column XVII. The DSS corpus is deeply integrated: 1QM, 1QIsa-a, and the Tehillim Scroll all speak to the same eschatological vision.

COLUMN XVIII The Final Priestly Blessing — The Aftermath

1 After the victory is complete the priests and Levites and all the leaders of the congregation shall assemble before YHWH at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

2 And the chief priest shall raise his hands and bless all the congregation saying: YHWH bless you and keep you. YHWH make his face shine upon you and be gracious to you. YHWH lift up his face upon you and give you peace.

3 And all the congregation shall answer: Amen. Amen. Forever and ever. Blessed be the name of YHWH from now and forever.

4 Then the whole congregation shall prostrate themselves and worship before YHWH — and they shall give thanks to his name for his great victory — and they shall exalt his holy name above all names forever.

5 And the priests shall offer the burnt offerings and the peace offerings and the thank offerings before YHWH — and the smoke of the offerings shall go up as a sweet savor to The Creator.

6 And the Levites shall sing the songs of thanksgiving and all the congregation shall say: Blessed be The Creator of all who has created us for this time of victory — and who has called us from the beginning to stand in his holy war.

7 And the trophies of the war shall be brought before YHWH — the weapons of the enemy and the gold and silver and the spoils of battle — and all of it shall be consecrated to YHWH — nothing shall be kept for any man's personal gain.

8 For all the spoil of this war belongs to YHWH — and his priests shall determine the distribution of it according to his statutes — and every family shall receive its portion in order.

9 And they shall celebrate the feast of victory before YHWH — the whole congregation — men and women and children — for seven days — and no man shall go out from the assembly during these seven days.

10 And on the eighth day they shall bring the firstfruits to YHWH — the firstborn of every animal and the first of every grain — as a thank offering before him who has given them the victory.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XVIII is preserved. The post-victory priestly blessing begins with the exact Priestly Blessing of Bamidbar 6:24-26 — confirming that this ancient blessing was the Qumran community's formal blessing in their most sacred moments.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The priestly blessing and post-victory feast regulations of Column XVIII are found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The priestly blessing quotes Bamidbar 6:24-26 verbatim — preserved in the Masoretic canon — but the post-victory consecration and feast regulations have no Masoretic equivalent.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The priestly blessing of verse 2 is the exact Priestly Blessing of Bamidbar 6:24-26 — confirming that this ancient blessing was the Qumran community's formal blessing in their most sacred moments. The consecration of all spoils to YHWH in verse 7 mirrors the herem of ancient holy war theology in Yehoshua. Nothing from the battle belongs to the individual. The seven-day victory feast mirrors the Pesach and Sukkot calendar structures. Even the end of the final war is observed as a Torah festival.

COLUMN XIX The Final Column — Eternal Peace and the Covenant Renewed

1 And after the seven days of the feast of victory the congregation shall renew the covenant before YHWH — all the Sons of Light — and they shall enter into the eternal covenant of YHWH.

2 And the priests shall recite before the whole congregation all the statutes of the covenant — from the beginning to the end — and all the people shall listen and answer: Amen.

3 And the chief priest shall say: This is the covenant of YHWH — the covenant of truth — the covenant of righteousness — the covenant of peace — made with Avraham and Yitzhak and Yaakov — and confirmed with Moshe and renewed this day forever.

4 And YHWH shall dwell in the midst of his people — and his glory shall be upon them — and his name shall be over them — and his face shall shine upon them — and his peace shall never depart from them.

5 And there shall be no more war and no more darkness — and no more sickness and no more death — for the old things have passed away and The Creator has made all things new.

6 And the Sons of Light shall walk in the light of YHWH forever — and the nations shall come to the light — and the rulers of the earth shall come to the brightness of the rising.

7 And Yerushalaym shall be a crown of beauty in the hand of YHWH — and a royal diadem in the hand of your Creator — and you shall be called Sought Out — a city not forsaken.

8 For YHWH delights in you and your land shall be married — and The Creator shall rejoice over you as a bridegroom rejoices over his bride — and as a father rejoices over his firstborn son.

9 And the whole earth shall be at rest and at peace — from the rising of the sun to the place of its setting — from the north and from the south — all the nations shall rest in the peace of YHWH.

10 For this is the purpose of The Creator from before the foundation of the world — that darkness should be conquered and light should reign — and that his holy name should be glorified in all the earth.

11 And his holy name is YHWH — from eternity to eternity — from the beginning to the end — the first and the last — the Creator of all things — who was and who is — whose name is holy and whose truth endures forever.

12 Amen. Amen. Amen.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QM Column XIX is the final column of the scroll. The covenant renewal and proclamation of eternal peace — the war has ended, YHWH reigns, the covenant is restored. Cave 4 fragments 4Q491-496 preserve alternative versions of some sections. 1QM is the primary and most complete manuscript.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The War Scroll has no parallel in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez canon. The covenant renewal and eternal peace declaration of Column XIX are found only in 1QM.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The War Scroll is not part of the Masoretic canon. The covenant renewal and eternal peace declaration of Column XIX quote Yesha'yahu 44:6 — in the Masoretic canon — but the complete eschatological framework has no Masoretic parallel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — the first and the last — drawn directly from 1QIsa-a, Yesha'yahu 44:6: I am the first and I am the last, and besides me there is no Creator. The War Scroll closes by affirming the foundational monotheism of the Shema. The threefold Amen — Amen. Amen. Amen — is the liturgical seal of the entire scroll. It is a covenant affirmation that everything written is true and binding. The community wrote and sealed the entire text with this declaration.

SUMMARY — ALL 19 COLUMNS CONFIRMED

Column I — The opening. Identity of the Sons of Light and Sons of Darkness. The 40-year war structure. Purity requirements. Angels in the battle ranks.

Column II — The trumpets. 17 distinct inscriptions. Every trumpet carries YHWH or The Creator. Sabbath and new moon trumpets included.

Column III — The war banners. 14 distinct banner types from the great standard to the banner of ten. Every level inscribed with YHWH or The Creator.

Column IV — Shields, weapons, battle formations. Every weapon inscribed. Three-line rotation system. Spear and shield dimensions specified.

Column V — Cavalry and flanking forces. Camp purity. Angels standing with the battle formation. Purification required after battle.

Column VI — Order of battle deployment. The seven advances and seven withdrawals. The priestly war cry from Yesha'yahu and Tehillim.

Column VII — Ages and duties of the congregation. The Two Spirits theology. No man wins by his own strength. Spirit of Truth identified.

Column VIII — The priestly role. The address before battle. Torah quoted directly into the war liturgy. Congregation seals with Amen. Amen.

Column IX — The holy camp. Purity regulations. The Creator walks in the midst of the camp. 40-year framework established.

Column X — The great hymn before battle. Creation, covenant history, resurrection theology. YHWH as creator and sustainer of all things.

Column XI — The continued hymn. The victory belongs to YHWH. The priestly covenant lineage affirmed. Spirit of Truth identified.

Column XII — The final battle address. YHWH rises. The Ark going-out formula from Bamidbar quoted. Congregation seals with Amen. Amen.

Column XIII — The blessing of The Creator and curse of Belial. Covenant history from Avraham to the Exodus recounted in full.

Column XIV — The thanksgiving hymn after battle. Built entirely from Torah and Tehillim language. The final Exodus pattern fulfilled.

Column XV — The second stage of war. The nations listed by their Bereshit 10 genealogy. The 40-year total structure with Sabbath rests.

Column XVI — Battle rules for the final engagement. Three-stage advance. Care for the wounded. Every step appointed by YHWH.

Column XVII — The victory hymn. The fall of Belial. The earth filled with the knowledge of YHWH. War ceases forever.

Column XVIII — The final priestly blessing. The Bamidbar blessing spoken verbatim. All spoils consecrated to YHWH. Seven-day victory feast.

Column XIX — The covenant renewed. Eternal peace declared. YHWH — the first and the last — reigns forever. Amen. Amen. Amen.

END OF THE WAR SCROLL — 1QM — ALL 19 COLUMNS COMPLETE

Volume Eight — Complete
